context: PyPI, the Python Package Index, launched in October 2002 as the official third-party software repository for Python. As of 2024, PyPI hosts over 500,000 packages from more than 900,000 contributors. The service is maintained by the Python Software Foundation.
question: When was PyPI launched and how many packages does it host?
expected_response: PyPI launched in October 2002. As of 2024, it hosts over 500,000 packages.

context: Three sorting algorithms are benchmarked on a 10,000 element list. Quicksort averages 2ms but has O(n^2) worst case. Mergesort averages 4ms with guaranteed O(n log n). Heapsort averages 5ms with O(n log n) worst case and no extra memory. The input distribution is unknown.
question: Which algorithm is the best choice when worst-case guarantees matter more than average speed?
expected_response: Mergesort is the best choice. Quicksort is fastest on average but has O(n^2) worst case, so it fails the guarantee requirement. Mergesort and heapsort both offer O(n log n) worst case; mergesort is faster on average.

context: The release notes for version 3.12 of a Python library cover three changes. First, the streaming API was rewritten to use asyncio generators instead of callbacks. Second, a new caching layer reduced repeat lookup latency from 40ms to under 1ms. Third, the deprecated v1 authentication flow was removed; users must migrate to v2 tokens.
question: Summarize the key changes in version 3.12.
expected_response: Three changes: the streaming API was rewritten to use asyncio generators, a new caching layer cut lookup latency from 40ms to under 1ms, and the deprecated v1 auth flow was removed in favor of v2 tokens.
